name: Angel Sound
description: Angel Cartel level 3 epic arc set in Curse nullsec. Three alternate entry agents (Minmatar, Amarr, or Cartel standings) funnel to K-QWHE; rewards +30% base Angel Cartel standing and a Cynabal blueprint copy.

missions.name: The Balance of Power
missions.briefing_html:
Hey, man.

If you're out here in a shithole like Egbinger, then maybe there's a chance you can help me out with something. We just had a bunch of Republic Fleet guys dock up and repair here. They'd been out in Curse dealing with some Angel Cartel raiders.

They were describing how the Cartel is having trouble right now with some other pirate groups. Seems there's a Guristas commander tearing them a new hole down there, as well as some Sansha meandering about causing problems. From the sounds of it, the Cartel's taking some significant losses.

Anyways, I was hoping you could fly down and check it out. While the Fleet was docked up, I had my boys go over their comms system. We've got ourselves the name of a Cartel agent involved in the invasion. Abdiel Verat.

It may sound strange to hear it, [you], but if you can find some way to deal with those Guristas and Sansha, I'd be thankful. The Cartel may give us grief, but they've done good things for the Minmatar people too, even if nobody has a damn clue why, or what's in it for them.

All I know is that I'd prefer the Cartel to Guristas thugs and Sansha freaks. If you can, go and deal with their problems. Just don't go helping them too much, got it?
missions.objective_notes: Arc entry via Minmatar standing; report to Abdiel Verat in K-QWHE — use interceptor for nullsec travel through potential bubble camps.

missions.name: Mistaken Identity
missions.briefing_html:
Oh, great, another one.

Look, I don't know where you're getting your intel, but I've got absolutely nothing to do with the Cartel. I'm not your “connection” in any sense of the word. Some scumbag was envious of my service record in Curse and decided to come up with that little story to explain away my success. You've been played, [you].

I don't know anything more about the Cartel than the next person. I have no idea about any pirate invasions that they could potentially need help with. And I certainly don't know that Abdiel Verat would be the person you should talk to if you wanted to know more.

As you can see, I can't help you, and even if I did have valuable information, it would be reported immediately to my superiors. I have a reputation to uphold.

Now, if you don't mind, I have important Navy matters to attend to. I hope you understand.
missions.objective_notes: Arc entry via Amarr standing; report to Abdiel Verat in K-QWHE — use interceptor for nullsec travel through potential bubble camps.

missions.name: Headhunted
missions.briefing_html:
A recruiter operating under the alias Ellar Stin approaches you in Konora, looking for capable capsuleers to join operations in Curse. She explains that the Angel Cartel is fighting off Guristas and Sansha incursions and needs fresh talent.

Stin warns that the journey to nullsec is genuinely dangerous — interdiction bubble camps and hostile alliances are a real threat — and strongly recommends flying an interceptor. She takes no responsibility for ships or implants lost along the way.

Your contact on arrival is Abdiel Verat, the Cartel's primary liaison for capsuleers in the region, stationed at the Archangels Assembly Plant in K-QWHE.
missions.objective_html: Report to Abdiel Verat at K-QWHE V - Moon 4 - Archangels Assembly Plant.
missions.objective_notes: Arc entry via Angel Cartel standing; report to Abdiel Verat in K-QWHE — use interceptor for nullsec travel through potential bubble camps.

missions.name: New Opportunities
missions.recommended_ship: T3 Destroyer or below (gate limit), EM/Thermal fit
missions.briefing_html:
Abdiel Verat, Angel Cartel.

I look out for talented people such as you who are interested in work. And times have certainly made our work interesting.

Since you're an egger, I'm guessing you know your way around wormholes, yes? While they've been a great aid for our scientists, they've also brought some problems to our doorstop, some of which is not good for business.

Our explorers have discovered a wormhole in a nearby system, but we cannot spare the manpower to lock it down. If you help us out by scouting the area, then I may have other more lucrative tasks to follow.
missions.objective_html: Recon the wormhole site for the Cartel

groups.label: Group 1 - Ahead at wormhole - 76 km - Killing is optional
groups.distance_text: Ahead at wormhole - 76 km - Killing is optional

groups.label: Group 2 - Wormhole - 76 km - Killing is optional
groups.trigger_text: Spawn Trigger: After 2 or 3 minutes

missions.objective_notes: Blitz: take gate and warp out — no kills required; room has 2–3 Sansha frigates and 2 elite cruisers but objective is recon only.

missions.name: Fight or Flight
missions.briefing_html:
At the moment I have more work than I have people, so you need to choose how you want to proceed.

We also have a shipment of important research material that needs to make it to Serpentis hands. The Inquest scientists in charge of the operation have been delayed, and moving the goods is a high-priority task we need someone to take over. We may also require an investigation into the reasons for the delay at Serpentis' end.

Both tasks need to be followed up. I don't care what you choose; we'll give one to you, and one to the next egger brave or stupid enough to show up down here.


Branch: Natural Consequences

Obviously, the Sansha need to be cleared from our wormhole site. If you can prove yourself on this, chances are I'll be able to request more assignments to give you. We have early reports of more problems abroad, so I'm sure we can keep you moving from target to target out there.

Post-choice:
Dislodging the Sansha won't be easy after they've already begun to settle themselves in, but we can focus on their weak points. If they're doing things by the book, the Sansha forces will have an Overseer there directing the group from a safe distance. Take out enough of the fodder vessels and you'll provoke its appearance. Once you destroy the Overseer their whole operation will fall apart. Think of it as a decapitation.

Branch: Serpentis Fill-In

Perhaps it's not worth the trouble to take down the Sansha encampment. They might have already gone in and destabilized the wormhole. We play by risk and reward, and the reward here looks like it'll be too low.

There's a high-priority shipment that has been sitting around waiting for someone capable enough to move it. It's headed to the Serpentis research facilities in Fountain, but Sarpati's crew is going to handle moving it from Curse over to their Headquarters. They were supposed to take it directly from this station but we've lost contact with the research team in charge of that operation. The job is yours if you want it. All you need to do is get it to nearby Serpentis Corp. transport hub, from there they'll handle the rest.

The cargo is small so you could easily fit it inside an interceptor or covert ops and I would suggest you do just that, [you]. I will not accept any excuses for the cargo's disappearance.
missions.objective_notes: Choose path: 'Serpentis Fill-In' leads to Chapter 2 Heaven; 'Natural Consequences' leads to Chapter 2 Utopia. No lasting impact on arc.

missions.name: Natural Consequences
missions.recommended_ship: T3 Destroyer or below (gate limit), EM/Thermal fit
missions.briefing_html:
Obviously, the Sansha need to be cleared from our wormhole site. If you can prove yourself on this, chances are I'll be able to request more assignments to give you. We have early reports of more problems abroad, so I'm sure we can keep you moving from target to target out there.

Post-choice:
Dislodging the Sansha won't be easy after they've already begun to settle themselves in, but we can focus on their weak points. If they're doing things by the book, the Sansha forces will have an Overseer there directing the group from a safe distance. Take out enough of the fodder vessels and you'll provoke its appearance. Once you destroy the Overseer their whole operation will fall apart. Think of it as a decapitation.
missions.objective_html: Eliminate the Sansha's Nation blockade and claim the wormhole for the Cartel.

missions.name: Serpentis Fill-In
missions.recommended_ship: Interceptor (nullsec travel, cargo is only 2 m³)
missions.briefing_html:
Perhaps it's not worth the trouble to take down the Sansha encampment. They might have already gone in and destabilized the wormhole. We play by risk and reward, and the reward here looks like it'll be too low.

There's a high-priority shipment that has been sitting around waiting for someone capable enough to move it. It's headed to the Serpentis research facilities in Fountain, but Sarpati's crew is going to handle moving it from Curse over to their Headquarters. They were supposed to take it directly from this station but we've lost contact with the research team in charge of that operation. The job is yours if you want it. All you need to do is get it to nearby Serpentis Corp. transport hub, from there they'll handle the rest.

The cargo is small so you could easily fit it inside an interceptor or covert ops and I would suggest you do just that, [you]. I will not accept any excuses for the cargo's disappearance.
missions.objective_html:
Deliver the Sealed Research Cache to the local Serpentis Transport Hub.
1x Sealed Research Cache (volume: 2 m3)


No information about the mission site.
missions.objective_notes: Pick up the Sealed Research Cache (2 m³) from station before undocking — easy to forget; deliver to Serpentis Transport Hub Container.

missions.name: Clear the Way
missions.recommended_ship: T3 Destroyer or below (gate limit), Kinetic/Thermal fit
missions.briefing_html:
Okay, the exploration team is away. If the Serpentis eggheads are right, they should arrive in Fountain securely and in record time.

Speak of the devil. I'm getting a report that one of our shipments coming back from Fountain just got hit by Guristas. It's always something.

There's a salvage crew nearby that can pick up the tech, but they aren't equipped to handle Guristas. If you clear out the Guristas, I can send in the recovery team. As always you'll be well paid.

groups.label: Group 2 - 30 km
groups.trigger_text: Spawn Trigger: Killing last of previous group

groups.label: Group 3 - 30 km
groups.trigger_text: Spawn Trigger: Killing last of previous group

groups.label: Group 4 - 30 km - Killing is optional
groups.trigger_text: Spawn Trigger: Killing last of previous group Neutral

missions.name: Ride to the Rescue
missions.recommended_ship: Assault Frigate or T3 Destroyer (Kinetic/Thermal fit)
missions.briefing_html:
Abdiel Verat has just learned why the research team never arrived to collect the goods you previously ferried: their labs are under siege by Guristas forces. A coordinated Guristas assault has overwhelmed the station's defenses, and time is running out.

Your task is to cut through to the research installation and eliminate Yukiro Demense, the Guristas commander leading the attack. Verat warns that Demense's frigate is fast and equipped to prevent escape.
missions.objective_html: Destroy Yukiro Demense and return to agent.
missions.objective_notes: Blitz: kill only Yukiro Demense (elite frigate); she webs and points — Kinetic weakest resist at 64%.

missions.name: Salvage Heist
missions.recommended_ship: T3 Destroyer or below (gate limit), Kinetic/Thermal fit
missions.briefing_html:
Our biggest competition in the Tech III race is not the Guristas or the Sansha. Hell, it's not even the empires. It's your kind. Capsuleers. I'll admit you guys have been giving us a run for our money. You're uniquely suited to wormhole space. Thankfully a few of you have been less than careful in normal space.

There's a capsuleer named Renton that makes his home nearby. We know he's been exploring wormhole space, and he's likely been stockpiling Sleeper tech at this base. He's only been a minor nuisance so far, but his tech cache could be useful. We would do the raid ourselves, but it's more effective to have capsuleers take care of each other, much less bloodshed.

Find Renton's base. Eliminate him. I'll be able to send an another recovery team once you've wiped out all hostiles.
missions.objective_html: An unaligned capsuleer, Gath Renton, emerged from one of the Angels' wormholes. Find and destroy him at his base.

groups.label: Group 2 - Left - 66 km - Killing is optional
Group 3 - 66 km - Killing is optional
groups.trigger_text: Spawn Trigger: Attacking Gath Renton

groups.label: Group 4 - 66 km - Killing is optional
groups.trigger_text: Spawn Trigger: Getting Gath Renton into armor

groups.label: Group 5 - 66 km - Killing is optional
groups.trigger_text: Spawn Trigger: Getting Gath Renton into structure

groups.label: Group 6 - Left - 66 km - Killing is optional

missions.objective_notes: Waves 1–3 triggered by damaging Gath Renton's shield, armor, and hull respectively — pull boss away from initial pack before shooting.

missions.name: The Best Kind of Revenge
missions.recommended_ship: Assault Frigate or T3 Destroyer (Kinetic/Thermal resist)
missions.briefing_html:
Verat reveals that Yukiro Demense has been exploiting a black market cloning facility in Curse whose administrator accepted bribes to keep it running. The license has since been revoked, but the facility remains operational and the personnel are complicit.

Your mission is to destroy all staff at the site and then upload a virus payload — sourced from Serpentis Inquest technology — to the Central Database Hub, ensuring no records survive. Demense's clone network must be severed at the root.
missions.objective_html: Eliminate all hostile targets, then approach the Central Database Hub to transmit the virus.

npcs.notes: Approach to complete mission — auto-transmits virus payload

missions.objective_notes: Do NOT destroy Cruise Missile Batteries — doing so triggers an additional -3.0% Guristas standing loss.

missions.name: Rabbit Trap
missions.recommended_ship: T3 Destroyer or below (gate limit), Omni or Kinetic focus for boss
missions.briefing_html:
All that's left to do is transport the tech you've helped us collect back up to Fountain. No, we're not asking you to haul. We've got a crew for that. Your job is more exciting.

We know there are Guristas still in the area, possibly with their Sansha pals. They want these parts badly enough to dare raiding in our territory. So, we decided to use their eagerness against them. We let the details leak about the timing of this shipment. They think they'll be ambushing a defenseless industrial. What they're going to find is you.

Kill every vessel that shows up.
missions.objective_html: Eliminate any attackers that show their face.

groups.label: Group 1 - Behind - 60 km
groups.trigger_text: Spawn Trigger: After 30 seconds Death Message An Ambush!Incoming hostiles!

groups.label: Group 2 - Behind - 60 km
groups.trigger_text: Spawn Trigger: Killing last of previous group

groups.label: Group 3 - Behind - 64 km
groups.trigger_text: Spawn Trigger: Killing last of previous group Spawn Message Yukiro Demen:I'm tired of your interference, egger. Time to die. Death Message Targets Eliminated:Good work, pilot. Our skies are clear. We'll be seeing you on the other side.

missions.objective_notes: Mission reward includes Domination Target Painter; boss Yukiro Demense (wave 3) webs -75% at 10 km, points to 24 km — Kinetic weakest resist.

missions.name: Wrath of Angels
missions.recommended_ship: Assault Frigate or T3 Destroyer (Kinetic/Thermal fit, long-range opener)
missions.briefing_html:
Verat informs you that Yukiro Demense is still operating in Curse and must be permanently dealt with. The implication is clear: killing the body is not enough — Demense's very clone line must be infected.

Upon completion, Verat confirms that Demense will be reactivated into a compromised clone, remarking that he has "contracted a disease which is going to follow him from clone to clone." The reward for this final act is a Domination-grade target painter.
missions.objective_html: Destroy Yukiro Demense and return to agent.

pockets.name: Room 1
pockets.notes: Keep Yukiro Demense at least 40 km from the Remote-Repair Facility before engaging.

npcs.notes: Repairs nearby ships — do not engage Yukiro Demense within 40 km of this structure

missions.objective_notes: Keep Yukiro Demense 40+ km from Remote-Repair Facility before attacking or it heals her; mission reward includes Domination Target Painter.

missions.name: Dominus
missions.briefing_html:
You've attracted the attention of Kadeja Dulche, one of the most important people in the Dominations. If he has a mission for you, the odds are good you'll be shooting some important people.

You best get going. I'd thank you for the assistance you provided, but an invitation to a meeting with a Domination agent says more than any words of mine will.
missions.objective_notes: Agent handoff — report to Kadeja Dulche at K-QWHE VII Moon 8 Dominations Assembly Plant to switch mission chains.

missions.name: The Lesser of Two
missions.recommended_ship: T3 Destroyer or below (gate limit for subsequent combat missions)
missions.briefing_html:
You've done well for us dealing with the Guristas and Sansha. Clashes with other frontier organizations come with the territory, however, and are never that much of a problem. The Archangels are more than capable of handling this aspect of daily operations. It is when other entities begin to stray outside their usual boundaries and establish themselves in our space that people begin to become rightly concerned. In these situations, we are called in to deal with them.

One Minmatar corporation, Boundless Creations, has made the mistake of conducting research in our space. They have come here on the misunderstanding that, just because their research is illegal, we will turn a blind eye to their presence. We've not corrected them on this false assumption. Rather, we've let them move in all of their assets and staff and slowly build themselves up. The time has now come to reveal to them where they truly stand with the Cartel.

Your mission is to destroy their research facility entirely, costing the interlopers billions. You can go about this whatever way you see fit. You can storm the facility head on, or weaken the defenses by knocking out their communications arrays first.

Either way, everyone in that place needs to be dealt with.
missions.objective_html:
Report to Kadeja Dulche


Branch: Data Destruction

Going for the straight shot? You're a pilot after my own heart.

The base is split into three sections. Each section contains a data center that houses all of Boundless Creations' illegal research. You must destroy these. Each data center serves as a redundant backup for the others, so it needs to be all or nothing.

The Cartel is putting a lot of trust in you. Make it happen.

Branch: Situation Normal

Boundless Creations has a communications array keeping tabs on the research base. If you attack the base now, they'll call in reinforcements and make your life difficult.

The good news is that they've attempted to hide the array inside a gas cloud rather than protect it in the open. They expect it to be undetected, and to most people it would be. Our scouts have uncovered the area though, and have observed engineers living at the compound. They will be carrying a security code which you must recover. You will be able to use it to deactivate the array without alerting the main Boundless Creation fleet.
missions.objective_notes: Choose path: 'Situation Normal' (two-mission stealth path, low standing loss) or 'Data Destruction' (direct one-mission assault, higher standing loss). No lasting impact.

missions.name: Data Destruction
missions.recommended_ship: T3 Destroyer or below (gate limit), Explosive/Kinetic fit

Going for the straight shot? You're a pilot after my own heart.

The base is split into three sections. Each section contains a data center that houses all of Boundless Creations' illegal research. You must destroy these. Each data center serves as a redundant backup for the others, so it needs to be all or nothing.

The Cartel is putting a lot of trust in you. Make it happen.
missions.objective_html: Destroy the Boundless Creations data centers.

missions.objective_notes: Kill all ships in rooms 1–2 to unlock gates (tooltip lies); room 3 only needs data center destroyed; incurs ~-0.24% Minmatar standing total.

missions.name: Situation Normal
missions.recommended_ship: T3 Destroyer or below (gate limit), Explosive/Kinetic fit
missions.briefing_html:
Boundless Creations has a communications array keeping tabs on the research base. If you attack the base now, they'll call in reinforcements and make your life difficult.

The good news is that they've attempted to hide the array inside a gas cloud rather than protect it in the open. They expect it to be undetected, and to most people it would be. Our scouts have uncovered the area though, and have observed engineers living at the compound. They will be carrying a security code which you must recover. You will be able to use it to deactivate the array without alerting the main Boundless Creation fleet.
missions.objective_html:
Eliminate the Boundless Creations engineers. Use their security codes to silence the communications array.


No information about the mission site.
missions.objective_notes: Kill Boundless Creations Engineers (durable Stabber/Bellicose hulls), loot Security Codes, place in Communications Array to complete remotely.

missions.name: Breaking the Lock
missions.recommended_ship: T3 Destroyer or below (gate limit), Explosive/Kinetic fit
missions.briefing_html:
With little time to spare, Verat explains that the Boundless Creations research base has been cut off from all support. The facility maintains three separate data centers, each acting as a backup for the others — all three must be destroyed to sever the network completely.

The site is defended by Minmatar Tribal warriors across three gated rooms, one jump from K-QWHE. Acceleration gate messages claiming the gates are unlocked are false; expect to fight through each room in sequence.
missions.objective_html: Destroy all three Boundless Creations Data Centers across three gated rooms.
missions.objective_notes: Three rooms of destroyers; gate tooltips lie — clear all ships to unlock each gate; destroying data centers costs -2.4% Minmatar Republic standing.

missions.name: Fear of Angels
missions.recommended_ship: Assault Frigate or T3 Destroyer (precision ammo, fast orbit to beat tracking)
missions.briefing_html:
Aren't you the lucky one? All the carnage you've inflicted on our enemies has caught the attention of SARO. They are the best that CONCORD has to offer, and they're not to be balked at.

This particular SARO pilot's name is Treonis, and she's been asking for you personally. My guess? Taking you out is her bid at a promotion.

Normally, we would launch a whole campaign to remove CONCORD scum from our space, but my superiors want to see you handle this situation. If you can destroy Treonis, I'm authorized to reward you with a single run blueprint for one of our rarer ships, the Cynabal.

Treonis is waiting for you by an abandoned colony in [system]. Destroy her, and the blueprint is yours.
missions.objective_notes: Solo vs Marshal Treonis (elite frigate, 27 m sig, 55% omni resists); loot CONCORD Star Emblem from wreck; use precision ammo; reward is Cynabal BPC.
